purpose: Authorize tool invocations against role policy, tool allowlists, and schema-constrained arguments so workflow agents can only execute approved capabilities.

endpoints.description: Authorize a tool call for a specific role and validated argument payload.

dependencies.purpose: Validate tool arguments against declared input schema before authorization completes

sidecar_specification.purpose: Authorize tool broker calls against role policy and input schema constraints.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use before invoking any external or internal tool through a brokered workflow boundary.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as the actual tool runner, rate limiter, or audit log writer.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Receive role, tool name, and argument payload. 2) Validate the arguments. 3) Approve only when the role and policy allow the tool invocation.

common_calls.description: Authorize a research search tool call

failure_modes.condition: Role is not permitted, tool is disallowed, or arguments fail schema validation
failure_modes.symptom: allowed false with a specific reason
failure_modes.recovery: Use an authorized role, fix the payload, or select an allowed tool

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Model authorization as a pure decision over role, tool metadata, and schema-validated arguments, with clear denial reasons for unauthorized role, forbidden tool, or invalid payload.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: If schema validation runs after authorization side effects, denials become ambiguous and unsafe. Validate arguments before approval leaves the broker boundary.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Use explicit allowlists per role and keep tool metadata separate from execution code.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not invoke the tool speculatively before authorization completes and do not hide denials behind generic errors.
